beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee, and light breakfast at home.
beats.reasoning: Early riser habit; Layla's energy is recovering from yesterday's low (35/100), and a quiet morning routine sets a grounded tone. No rush since transit strike means altered commute logistics.

beats.activity: Walk to Lincoln High School, film camera in bag.
beats.location_name: Lincoln High School (South Lake Union, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: South Lake Union to SLU workplace is walkable (~1.5 km); transit strike is irrelevant for this short neighborhood route. Layla walks everywhere when possible and brings her camera.

beats.activity: Teach morning and midday science classes; prep labs and grade quizzes.
beats.location_name: Lincoln High School (South Lake Union, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: High school teaching day; standard morning-to-early-afternoon block. Mild weather and low smoke (yesterday's apocalyptic haze clearing) means students likely more engaged.

beats.activity: Lunch at Molly Moon's, grab ice cream and sit in the AC.
beats.location_name: Molly Moon's Homemade Ice Cream (Capitol Hill, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Established pattern from recent days; ~1.5 km walk from school is manageable in partly cloudy 68F weather. Layla values the ritual and the air conditioning. Transit strike doesn't affect walking.

beats.activity: Walk back to school; catch a moment shooting photos of birds or light near Capitol Hill.
beats.location_name: Lincoln High School (South Lake Union, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Return walk gives Layla time with her film camera in good daylight; partly cloudy conditions are ideal for photography. She carries the camera everywhere and uses walking time to scout shots.

beats.activity: Teach afternoon chemistry and biology classes; supervise lab work.
beats.location_name: Lincoln High School (South Lake Union, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Afternoon teaching block; students likely more engaged with mild weather. Layla finds satisfaction in moments when students 'get it,' as noted in her recent diary.

beats.activity: Walk home from school; stop by neighborhood to scout for birds or light.
beats.reasoning: Transit strike still in effect (ends at 4pm, but walking is faster anyway for SLU-to-SLU). Partly cloudy light is good for birdwatching and photography; Layla often takes detours with her camera.

beats.activity: Decompress at home: change clothes, review brewing notes, prep ingredients for next batch.
beats.reasoning: Layla uses home time to recover energy and engage in her grounding hobby (home brewing). This fits her pattern of decompressing after work and planning evening activities around mood and weather.

beats.activity: Meet Maya at Pike Place Market for early evening catch-up and errand.
beats.reasoning: Layla values close friendships and has been planning with Maya; partly cloudy weather is pleasant for the market. ~2 km walk from home (or short ride now that surge pricing is in effect post-4pm strike end).

beats.activity: Walk back home, stopping to photograph evening light near the water or neighborhood.
beats.reasoning: Golden hour light at 7–8pm in June is perfect for Layla's film camera; she carries it everywhere and uses commute time to shoot. Gives her quiet time to transition from social energy to evening wind-down.

beats.activity: Dinner, home brewing prep, journaling, and light reading.
beats.reasoning: Quiet evening at home reflects Layla's recovering energy (35/100 yesterday) and her preference for grounding solo activities. Brewing is meditative; journaling and reading are part of her wind-down routine.
8-hour sleep block, ending before 6:30am to allow for early-riser pattern tomorrow. Layla is settled and at home, ready for restorative rest.

diary: Woke early and walked to Lincoln feeling grateful the transit strike didn't derail my day—turns out my feet are the most reliable transit in this city. Morning classes were solid; the kids nailed their lab setup. Around midday I needed a break, so I ducked over to Capitol Hill and grabbed ice cream at Molly Moon's, sitting in the AC watching people rush past. The walk back felt long but I caught some beautiful light near the water, so I stopped to shoot a few frames. Afternoon classes kept me busy, then I met Maya at Pike Place for coffee and some actual human conversation—we haven't caught up properly in weeks. By the time I got home, I was pleasantly tired. Spent the evening prepping for my next brewing batch, journaling about the day, reading a bit. There's something grounding about a day that stays mostly local, mostly on foot. Feels like I actually *saw* the city today instead of just passing through it.
thought_process: The transit strike initially felt like a problem, but I realized pretty quickly that walking to school is only twenty minutes—I've done it a hundred times. It actually gave me space to breathe this morning instead of cramming onto a bus. I chose Molly Moon's for lunch because I needed air conditioning and a moment to myself; the kids had been wound up all morning. The photography breaks felt important too; I grabbed my film camera this morning deliberately, sensing I'd want those pauses. Meeting Maya was non-negotiable—we'd been texting about it for days. By evening, I felt genuinely settled rather than just tired. The brewing prep centered me. I think the lack of transit chaos actually slowed my pace in a good way; I wasn't rushing between destinations, just... moving through the neighborhood intentionally.
world_event_prompt: Mild, partly cloudy Thursday, high of 68F. Citywide transit strike: all Metro buses and Link Light Rail suspended until 4pm. Rideshare surge pricing in effect.
